documentation:
libclang-for-pip
================

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/libclang)](https://pypi.org/project/libclang)
![Python](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/libclang)
![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/libclang)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/libclang)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/blob/master/LICENSE.TXT)

[![Arch: x86\_64](https://img.shields.io/badge/arch-x86__64-brightgreen)](https://pypi.org/project/libclang/#files)
[![Arch: aarch64](https://img.shields.io/badge/arch-aarch64-yellowgreen)](https://pypi.org/project/libclang/#files)
[![Arch: arm](https://img.shields.io/badge/arch-arm-orange)](https://pypi.org/project/libclang/#files)

[![Linux](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/workflows/libclang-linux-amd64/badge.svg)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/actions/workflows/libclang-linux-amd64.yml)
[![Linux Arm](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/workflows/libclang-linux-arm/badge.svg)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/actions/workflows/libclang-linux-arm.yml)
[![Linux AArch64](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/workflows/libclang-linux-aarch64/badge.svg)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/actions/workflows/libclang-linux-aarch64.yml)
[![Linux Alpine](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/workflows/libclang-alpine-amd64/badge.svg)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/actions/workflows/libclang-alpine-amd64.yml)

[![MacOS Intel](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/workflows/libclang-macosx-amd64/badge.svg)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/actions/workflows/libclang-macosx-amd64.yml)
[![MacOS M1](https://img.shields.io/cirrus/github/sighingnow/libclang?label=libclang-macosx-arm64)](https://cirrus-ci.com/github/sighingnow/libclang)

[![Windows](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/workflows/libclang-windows-amd64/badge.svg)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/actions/workflows/libclang-windows-amd64.yml)
[![Windows AArch64](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/workflows/libclang-windows-aarch64/badge.svg)](https://github.com/sighingnow/libclang/actions/workflows/libclang-windows-aarch64.yml)

The repository contains code taken from [the LLVM project][1], to make it easier to install
clang's python bindings.

The repository copies necessary Python binding files from LLVM repo, adds packaging scripts
to make it a valid Python package and finally uploads the package to [pypi][2]. To make the libclang
available without installing the LLVM toolkits, this package provides bundled static-linked libclang
shared library for different platforms, which, should work well on OSX, Windows, as well as
usual Linux distributions.

The aim of this project is to make the `clang.cindex` (aka., Clang Python Bindings)
available for more Python users, without setting up the LLVM environment. To install the package,
you just need to run

```bash
pip install libclang
```

Note that the library is named `libclang`, the package `clang` on PyPi is another package and
doesn't bundle the prebuilt shared library.

Internals
---------

Update class variable `library_path` of `Config` in `cindex.py` as:

```python
    library_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)), 'native')
```

License
-------

This repository follows the license agreement of the LLVM project, see [Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception](./LICENSE.TXT).

[1]: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/tree/main/clang/bindings/python
[2]: https://pypi.org/project/libclang

modules.name: python.clang.native
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: python.clang.enumerations
documentation:
Clang Enumerations
==================

This module provides static definitions of enumerations that exist in libclang.

Enumerations are typically defined as a list of tuples. The exported values are
typically munged into other types or classes at module load time.

All enumerations are centrally defined in this file so they are all grouped
together and easier to audit. And, maybe even one day this file will be
automatically generated by scanning the libclang headers!
===- enumerations.py - Python Enumerations ------------------*- python -*--===#
Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.
See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception

modules.name: setup

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: python.clang
documentation:
Clang Library Bindings
======================

This package provides access to the Clang compiler and libraries.

The available modules are:

  cindex

    Bindings for the Clang indexing library.
===- __init__.py - Clang Python Bindings --------------------*- python -*--===#
Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.
See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception

modules.name: python.clang.cindex
documentation:
Clang Indexing Library Bindings
===============================

This module provides an interface to the Clang indexing library. It is a
low-level interface to the indexing library which attempts to match the Clang
API directly while also being "pythonic". Notable differences from the C API
are:

 * string results are returned as Python strings, not CXString objects.

 * null cursors are translated to None.

 * access to child cursors is done via iteration, not visitation.

The major indexing objects are:

  Index

    The top-level object which manages some global library state.

  TranslationUnit

    High-level object encapsulating the AST for a single translation unit. These
    can be loaded from .ast files or parsed on the fly.

  Cursor

    Generic object for representing a node in the AST.

  SourceRange, SourceLocation, and File

    Objects representing information about the input source.

Most object information is exposed using properties, when the underlying API
call is efficient.
===- cindex.py - Python Indexing Library Bindings -----------*- python -*--===#
Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.
See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception

functions.name: python.clang.cindex.register_functions
documentation:
Register function prototypes with a libclang library instance.

This must be called as part of library instantiation so Python knows how
to call out to the shared library.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.c_interop_string
documentation: Python 3 strings are unicode, translate them to/from utf8 for C-interop.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnitLoadError
documentation:
Represents an error that occurred when loading a TranslationUnit.

This is raised in the case where a TranslationUnit could not be
instantiated due to failure in the libclang library.

FIXME: Make libclang expose additional error information in this scenario.
## Exception Classes ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnitSaveError
documentation:
Represents an error that occurred when saving a TranslationUnit.

Each error has associated with it an enumerated value, accessible under
e.save_error. Consumers can compare the value with one of the ERROR_
constants in this class.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.CachedProperty
documentation:
Decorator that lazy-loads the value of a property.

The first time the property is accessed, the original property function is
executed. The value it returns is set as the new value of that instance's
property, replacing the original method.
## Structures and Utility Classes ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex._CXString
documentation: Helper for transforming CXString results.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation.from_position
documentation:
Retrieve the source location associated with a given file/line/column in
a particular translation unit.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation.from_offset
documentation:
Retrieve a SourceLocation from a given character offset.

tu -- TranslationUnit file belongs to
file -- File instance to obtain offset from
offset -- Integer character offset within file

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation.file
documentation: Get the file represented by this source location.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation.line
documentation: Get the line represented by this source location.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation.column
documentation: Get the column represented by this source location.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation.offset
documentation: Get the file offset represented by this source location.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceLocation.is_in_system_header
documentation: Returns true if the given source location is in a system header.
A SourceLocation represents a particular location within a source file.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceRange

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceRange.from_locations
documentation: FIXME: Eliminate this and make normal constructor? Requires hiding ctypes
object.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceRange.start
documentation:
Return a SourceLocation representing the first character within a
source range.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceRange.end
documentation:
Return a SourceLocation representing the last character within a
source range.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.SourceRange.__contains__
documentation: Useful to detect the Token/Lexer bug

A SourceRange describes a range of source locations within the source
code.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.Diagnostic

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Diagnostic.category_number
documentation: The category number for this diagnostic or 0 if unavailable.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Diagnostic.category_name
documentation: The string name of the category for this diagnostic.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Diagnostic.option
documentation: The command-line option that enables this diagnostic.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Diagnostic.disable_option
documentation: The command-line option that disables this diagnostic.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Diagnostic.format
documentation:
Format this diagnostic for display. The options argument takes
Diagnostic.Display* flags, which can be combined using bitwise OR. If
the options argument is not provided, the default display options will
be used.

A Diagnostic is a single instance of a Clang diagnostic. It includes the
diagnostic severity, the message, the location the diagnostic occurred, as
well as additional source ranges and associated fix-it hints.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.FixIt
documentation:
A FixIt represents a transformation to be applied to the source to
"fix-it". The fix-it should be applied by replacing the given source range
with the given value.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TokenGroup

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TokenGroup.get_tokens
documentation:
Helper method to return all tokens in an extent.

This functionality is needed multiple places in this module. We define
it here because it seems like a logical place.

Helper class to facilitate token management.

Tokens are allocated from libclang in chunks. They must be disposed of as a
collective group.

One purpose of this class is for instances to represent groups of allocated
tokens. Each token in a group contains a reference back to an instance of
this class. When all tokens from a group are garbage collected, it allows
this class to be garbage collected. When this class is garbage collected,
it calls the libclang destructor which invalidates all tokens in the group.

You should not instantiate this class outside of this module.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TokenKind

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TokenKind.__init__
documentation: Create a new TokenKind instance from a numeric value and a name.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TokenKind.from_value
documentation: Obtain a registered TokenKind instance from its value.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TokenKind.register
documentation:
Register a new TokenKind enumeration.

This should only be called at module load time by code within this
package.

class_variables.name: python.clang.cindex.TokenKind._value_map
documentation: int -> TokenKind
Describes a specific type of a Token.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.BaseEnumeration

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.BaseEnumeration.name
documentation: Get the enumeration name of this cursor kind.

Common base class for named enumerations held in sync with Index.h values.

Subclasses must define their own _kinds and _name_map members, as:
_kinds = []
_name_map = None
These values hold the per-subclass instances and value-to-name mappings,
respectively.
## Cursor Kinds ###

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.get_all_kinds
documentation: Return all CursorKind enumeration instances.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_declaration
documentation: Test if this is a declaration kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_reference
documentation: Test if this is a reference kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_expression
documentation: Test if this is an expression kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_statement
documentation: Test if this is a statement kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_attribute
documentation: Test if this is an attribute kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_invalid
documentation: Test if this is an invalid kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_translation_unit
documentation: Test if this is a translation unit kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_preprocessing
documentation: Test if this is a preprocessing kind.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CursorKind.is_unexposed
documentation: Test if this is an unexposed kind.
A CursorKind describes the kind of entity that a cursor points to.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TemplateArgumentKind
documentation:
A TemplateArgumentKind describes the kind of entity that a template argument
represents.
## Template Argument Kinds ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.ExceptionSpecificationKind
documentation:
An ExceptionSpecificationKind describes the kind of exception specification
that a function has.
## Exception Specification Kinds ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_definition
documentation:
Returns true if the declaration pointed at by the cursor is also a
definition of that entity.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_const_method
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ member function or member
function template that is declared 'const'.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_converting_constructor
documentation: Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ converting constructor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_copy_constructor
documentation: Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ copy constructor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_default_constructor
documentation: Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ default constructor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_move_constructor
documentation: Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ move constructor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_default_method
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ member function or member
function template that is declared '= default'.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_deleted_method
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ member function or member
function template that is declared '= delete'.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_copy_assignment_operator_method
python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_move_assignment_operator_method
python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_explicit_method
python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_mutable_field
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ field that is declared
'mutable'.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_pure_virtual_method
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ member function or member
function template that is declared pure virtual.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_static_method
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ member function or member
function template that is declared 'static'.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_virtual_method
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ member function or member
function template that is declared 'virtual'.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_abstract_record
documentation:
Returns True if the cursor refers to a C++ record declaration
that has pure virtual member functions.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_scoped_enum
documentation: Returns True if the cursor refers to a scoped enum declaration.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_definition
documentation:
If the cursor is a reference to a declaration or a declaration of
some entity, return a cursor that points to the definition of that
entity.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_usr
documentation:
Return the Unified Symbol Resolution (USR) for the entity referenced
by the given cursor (or None).

A Unified Symbol Resolution (USR) is a string that identifies a
particular entity (function, class, variable, etc.) within a
program. USRs can be compared across translation units to determine,
e.g., when references in one translation refer to an entity defined in
another translation unit.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_included_file
documentation: Returns the File that is included by the current inclusion cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.kind
documentation: Return the kind of this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.spelling
documentation: Return the spelling of the entity pointed at by the cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.displayname
documentation:
Return the display name for the entity referenced by this cursor.

The display name contains extra information that helps identify the
cursor, such as the parameters of a function or template or the
arguments of a class template specialization.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.mangled_name
documentation: Return the mangled name for the entity referenced by this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.location
documentation:
Return the source location (the starting character) of the entity
pointed at by the cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.linkage
documentation: Return the linkage of this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.tls_kind
documentation: Return the thread-local storage (TLS) kind of this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.extent
documentation:
Return the source range (the range of text) occupied by the entity
pointed at by the cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.storage_class
documentation:
Retrieves the storage class (if any) of the entity pointed at by the
cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.availability
documentation: Retrieves the availability of the entity pointed at by the cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.access_specifier
documentation:
Retrieves the access specifier (if any) of the entity pointed at by the
cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.type
documentation: Retrieve the Type (if any) of the entity pointed at by the cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.canonical
documentation:
Return the canonical Cursor corresponding to this Cursor.

The canonical cursor is the cursor which is representative for the
underlying entity. For example, if you have multiple forward
declarations for the same class, the canonical cursor for the forward
declarations will be identical.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.result_type
documentation: Retrieve the Type of the result for this Cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.exception_specification_kind
documentation:
Retrieve the exception specification kind, which is one of the values
from the ExceptionSpecificationKind enumeration.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.underlying_typedef_type
documentation:
Return the underlying type of a typedef declaration.

Returns a Type for the typedef this cursor is a declaration for. If
the current cursor is not a typedef, this raises.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.enum_type
documentation:
Return the integer type of an enum declaration.

Returns a Type corresponding to an integer. If the cursor is not for an
enum, this raises.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.enum_value
documentation: Return the value of an enum constant.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.objc_type_encoding
documentation: Return the Objective-C type encoding as a str.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.hash
documentation: Returns a hash of the cursor as an int.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.semantic_parent
documentation: Return the semantic parent for this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.lexical_parent
documentation: Return the lexical parent for this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.translation_unit
documentation: Returns the TranslationUnit to which this Cursor belongs.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.referenced
documentation:
For a cursor that is a reference, returns a cursor
representing the entity that it references.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.brief_comment
documentation: Returns the brief comment text associated with that Cursor

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.raw_comment
documentation: Returns the raw comment text associated with that Cursor

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_arguments
documentation: Return an iterator for accessing the arguments of this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_num_template_arguments
documentation: Returns the number of template args associated with this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_template_argument_kind
documentation:
Returns the TemplateArgumentKind for the indicated template
argument.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_template_argument_type
documentation: Returns the CXType for the indicated template argument.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_template_argument_value
documentation: Returns the value of the indicated arg as a signed 64b integer.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_template_argument_unsigned_value
documentation: Returns the value of the indicated arg as an unsigned 64b integer.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_children
documentation: Return an iterator for accessing the children of this cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.walk_preorder
documentation:
Depth-first preorder walk over the cursor and its descendants.

Yields cursors.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_tokens
documentation:
Obtain Token instances formulating that compose this Cursor.

This is a generator for Token instances. It returns all tokens which
occupy the extent this cursor occupies.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_field_offsetof
documentation: Returns the offsetof the FIELD_DECL pointed by this Cursor.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_anonymous
documentation: Check if the record is anonymous.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.is_bitfield
documentation: Check if the field is a bitfield.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Cursor.get_bitfield_width
documentation: Retrieve the width of a bitfield.

The Cursor class represents a reference to an element within the AST. It
acts as a kind of iterator.
## Cursors ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.StorageClass

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.StorageClass.name
documentation: Get the enumeration name of this storage class.
Describes the storage class of a declaration

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.AvailabilityKind
documentation: Describes the availability of an entity.
## Availability Kinds ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.AccessSpecifier
documentation: Describes the access of a C++ class member
## C++ access specifiers ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TypeKind

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TypeKind.spelling
documentation: Retrieve the spelling of this TypeKind.
Describes the kind of type.
## Type Kinds ###

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.RefQualifierKind
documentation: Describes a specific ref-qualifier of a type.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.LinkageKind
documentation: Describes the kind of linkage of a cursor.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TLSKind
documentation: Describes the kind of thread-local storage (TLS) of a cursor.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.Type

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.kind
documentation: Return the kind of this type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.argument_types
documentation:
Retrieve a container for the non-variadic arguments for this type.

The returned object is iterable and indexable. Each item in the
container is a Type instance.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.element_type
documentation:
Retrieve the Type of elements within this Type.

If accessed on a type that is not an array, complex, or vector type, an
exception will be raised.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.element_count
documentation:
Retrieve the number of elements in this type.

Returns an int.

If the Type is not an array or vector, this raises.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.translation_unit
documentation: The TranslationUnit to which this Type is associated.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_canonical
documentation:
Return the canonical type for a Type.

Clang's type system explicitly models typedefs and all the
ways a specific type can be represented.  The canonical type
is the underlying type with all the "sugar" removed.  For
example, if 'T' is a typedef for 'int', the canonical type for
'T' would be 'int'.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.is_const_qualified
documentation:
Determine whether a Type has the "const" qualifier set.

This does not look through typedefs that may have added "const"
at a different level.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.is_volatile_qualified
documentation:
Determine whether a Type has the "volatile" qualifier set.

This does not look through typedefs that may have added "volatile"
at a different level.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.is_restrict_qualified
documentation:
Determine whether a Type has the "restrict" qualifier set.

This does not look through typedefs that may have added "restrict" at
a different level.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.is_function_variadic
documentation: Determine whether this function Type is a variadic function type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.is_pod
documentation: Determine whether this Type represents plain old data (POD).

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_pointee
documentation: For pointer types, returns the type of the pointee.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_declaration
documentation: Return the cursor for the declaration of the given type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_result
documentation: Retrieve the result type associated with a function type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_array_element_type
documentation: Retrieve the type of the elements of the array type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_array_size
documentation: Retrieve the size of the constant array.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_class_type
documentation: Retrieve the class type of the member pointer type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_named_type
documentation: Retrieve the type named by the qualified-id.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_align
documentation: Retrieve the alignment of the record.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_size
documentation: Retrieve the size of the record.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_offset
documentation: Retrieve the offset of a field in the record.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_ref_qualifier
documentation: Retrieve the ref-qualifier of the type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_fields
documentation: Return an iterator for accessing the fields of this type.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.get_exception_specification_kind
documentation:
Return the kind of the exception specification; a value from
the ExceptionSpecificationKind enumeration.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Type.spelling
documentation: Retrieve the spelling of this Type.
The type of an element in the abstract syntax tree.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.ClangObject
documentation:
A helper for Clang objects. This class helps act as an intermediary for
the ctypes library and the Clang CIndex library.
# CIndex Objects ##
CIndex objects (derived from ClangObject) are essentially lightweight
wrappers attached to some underlying object, which is exposed via CIndex as
a void*.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex._CXUnsavedFile
documentation: Helper for passing unsaved file arguments.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CompletionChunk.__kindNumber
documentation: We do not use @CachedProperty here, as the manual implementation is
apparently still significantly faster. Please profile carefully if you
would like to add CachedProperty back.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.Index

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Index.create
documentation:
Create a new Index.
Parameters:
excludeDecls -- Exclude local declarations from translation units.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Index.read
documentation: Load a TranslationUnit from the given AST file.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Index.parse
documentation:
Load the translation unit from the given source code file by running
clang and generating the AST before loading. Additional command line
parameters can be passed to clang via the args parameter.

In-memory contents for files can be provided by passing a list of pairs
to as unsaved_files, the first item should be the filenames to be mapped
and the second should be the contents to be substituted for the
file. The contents may be passed as strings or file objects.

If an error was encountered during parsing, a TranslationUnitLoadError
will be raised.

The Index type provides the primary interface to the Clang CIndex library,
primarily by providing an interface for reading and parsing translation
units.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.from_source
documentation:
Create a TranslationUnit by parsing source.

This is capable of processing source code both from files on the
filesystem as well as in-memory contents.

Command-line arguments that would be passed to clang are specified as
a list via args. These can be used to specify include paths, warnings,
etc. e.g. ["-Wall", "-I/path/to/include"].

In-memory file content can be provided via unsaved_files. This is an
iterable of 2-tuples. The first element is the filename (str or
PathLike). The second element defines the content. Content can be
provided as str source code or as file objects (anything with a read()
method). If a file object is being used, content will be read until EOF
and the read cursor will not be reset to its original position.

options is a bitwise or of TranslationUnit.PARSE_XXX flags which will
control parsing behavior.

index is an Index instance to utilize. If not provided, a new Index
will be created for this TranslationUnit.

To parse source from the filesystem, the filename of the file to parse
is specified by the filename argument. Or, filename could be None and
the args list would contain the filename(s) to parse.

To parse source from an in-memory buffer, set filename to the virtual
filename you wish to associate with this source (e.g. "test.c"). The
contents of that file are then provided in unsaved_files.

If an error occurs, a TranslationUnitLoadError is raised.

Please note that a TranslationUnit with parser errors may be returned.
It is the caller's responsibility to check tu.diagnostics for errors.

Also note that Clang infers the source language from the extension of
the input filename. If you pass in source code containing a C++ class
declaration with the filename "test.c" parsing will fail.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.from_ast_file
documentation:
Create a TranslationUnit instance from a saved AST file.

A previously-saved AST file (provided with -emit-ast or
TranslationUnit.save()) is loaded from the filename specified.

If the file cannot be loaded, a TranslationUnitLoadError will be
raised.

index is optional and is the Index instance to use. If not provided,
a default Index will be created.

filename can be str or PathLike.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.__init__
documentation:
Create a TranslationUnit instance.

TranslationUnits should be created using one of the from_* @classmethod
functions above. __init__ is only called internally.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.cursor
documentation: Retrieve the cursor that represents the given translation unit.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.spelling
documentation: Get the original translation unit source file name.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.get_includes
documentation:
Return an iterable sequence of FileInclusion objects that describe the
sequence of inclusions in a translation unit. The first object in
this sequence is always the input file. Note that this method will not
recursively iterate over header files included through precompiled
headers.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.get_file
documentation: Obtain a File from this translation unit.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.get_location
documentation:
Obtain a SourceLocation for a file in this translation unit.

The position can be specified by passing:

  - Integer file offset. Initial file offset is 0.
  - 2-tuple of (line number, column number). Initial file position is
    (0, 0)

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.get_extent
documentation:
Obtain a SourceRange from this translation unit.

The bounds of the SourceRange must ultimately be defined by a start and
end SourceLocation. For the locations argument, you can pass:

  - 2 SourceLocation instances in a 2-tuple or list.
  - 2 int file offsets via a 2-tuple or list.
  - 2 2-tuple or lists of (line, column) pairs in a 2-tuple or list.

e.g.

get_extent('foo.c', (5, 10))
get_extent('foo.c', ((1, 1), (1, 15)))

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.diagnostics
documentation: Return an iterable (and indexable) object containing the diagnostics.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.reparse
documentation:
Reparse an already parsed translation unit.

In-memory contents for files can be provided by passing a list of pairs
as unsaved_files, the first items should be the filenames to be mapped
and the second should be the contents to be substituted for the
file. The contents may be passed as strings or file objects.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.save
documentation:
Saves the TranslationUnit to a file.

This is equivalent to passing -emit-ast to the clang frontend. The
saved file can be loaded back into a TranslationUnit. Or, if it
corresponds to a header, it can be used as a pre-compiled header file.

If an error occurs while saving, a TranslationUnitSaveError is raised.
If the error was TranslationUnitSaveError.ERROR_INVALID_TU, this means
the constructed TranslationUnit was not valid at time of save. In this
case, the reason(s) why should be available via
TranslationUnit.diagnostics().

filename -- The path to save the translation unit to (str or PathLike).

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.codeComplete
documentation:
Code complete in this translation unit.

In-memory contents for files can be provided by passing a list of pairs
as unsaved_files, the first items should be the filenames to be mapped
and the second should be the contents to be substituted for the
file. The contents may be passed as strings or file objects.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.TranslationUnit.get_tokens
documentation:
Obtain tokens in this translation unit.

This is a generator for Token instances. The caller specifies a range
of source code to obtain tokens for. The range can be specified as a
2-tuple of SourceLocation or as a SourceRange. If both are defined,
behavior is undefined.

Represents a source code translation unit.

This is one of the main types in the API. Any time you wish to interact
with Clang's representation of a source file, you typically start with a
translation unit.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.File

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.File.from_name
documentation: Retrieve a file handle within the given translation unit.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.File.name
documentation: Return the complete file and path name of the file.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.File.time
documentation: Return the last modification time of the file.

The File class represents a particular source file that is part of a
translation unit.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.FileInclusion

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.FileInclusion.is_input_file
documentation: True if the included file is the input file.

The FileInclusion class represents the inclusion of one source file by
another via a '#include' directive or as the input file for the translation
unit. This class provides information about the included file, the including
file, the location of the '#include' directive and the depth of the included
file in the stack. Note that the input file has depth 0.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.CompilationDatabaseError
documentation:
Represents an error that occurred when working with a CompilationDatabase

Each error is associated to an enumerated value, accessible under
e.cdb_error. Consumers can compare the value with one of the ERROR_
constants in this class.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.CompileCommand

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CompileCommand.directory
documentation: Get the working directory for this CompileCommand

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CompileCommand.filename
documentation: Get the working filename for this CompileCommand

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CompileCommand.arguments
documentation:
Get an iterable object providing each argument in the
command line for the compiler invocation as a _CXString.

Invariant : the first argument is the compiler executable
Represents the compile command used to build a file

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.CompileCommands
documentation:
CompileCommands is an iterable object containing all CompileCommand
that can be used for building a specific file.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.CompilationDatabase

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CompilationDatabase.fromDirectory
documentation: Builds a CompilationDatabase from the database found in buildDir

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CompilationDatabase.getCompileCommands
documentation:
Get an iterable object providing all the CompileCommands available to
build filename. Returns None if filename is not found in the database.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.CompilationDatabase.getAllCompileCommands
documentation:
Get an iterable object providing all the CompileCommands available from
the database.

The CompilationDatabase is a wrapper class around
clang::tooling::CompilationDatabase

It enables querying how a specific source file can be built.

classes.name: python.clang.cindex.Token

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Token.spelling
documentation:
The spelling of this token.

This is the textual representation of the token in source.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Token.kind
documentation: Obtain the TokenKind of the current token.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Token.location
documentation: The SourceLocation this Token occurs at.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Token.extent
documentation: The SourceRange this Token occupies.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Token.cursor
documentation: The Cursor this Token corresponds to.

Represents a single token from the preprocessor.

Tokens are effectively segments of source code. Source code is first parsed
into tokens before being converted into the AST and Cursors.

Tokens are obtained from parsed TranslationUnit instances. You currently
can't create tokens manually.

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Config.set_library_path
documentation: Set the path in which to search for libclang

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Config.set_library_file
documentation: Set the exact location of libclang

methods.name: python.clang.cindex.Config.set_compatibility_check
documentation:
Perform compatibility check when loading libclang

The python bindings are only tested and evaluated with the version of
libclang they are provided with. To ensure correct behavior a (limited)
compatibility check is performed when loading the bindings. This check
will throw an exception, as soon as it fails.

In case these bindings are used with an older version of libclang, parts
that have been stable between releases may still work. Users of the
python bindings can disable the compatibility check. This will cause
the python bindings to load, even though they are written for a newer
version of libclang. Failures now arise if unsupported or incompatible
features are accessed. The user is required to test themselves if the
features they are using are available and compatible between different
libclang versions.
